Thought 3: The timeline strongly points to Stuart. He was alone at the chalet during the day when the murder occurred (as indicated by the power outage lasting all day and his presence there). The severed power cables, which are his signature method, were found at the scene. While Alice has a strong motive (Cecilia had blackmail material on her), her opportunity is less direct—she was taking axes in the evening and was at home, but the murder happened during the day when Stuart was on site. The evidence of Stuart's resentment and his presence alone at the crime scene makes him the most likely murderer.
